key_insight: The paper derives a closed-form vector-geometry model for muon-ring formation in dual-mirror Cherenkov telescopes, enabling more accurate calibration that includes secondary-mirror and camera shadowing effects.
engineering_relevance: This improves optical-throughput and point-spread-function calibration for CTAO telescopes, especially Schwarzschild-Couder designs where current methods can miss large inclination-dependent biases.
method: Built an analytical muon-ring model using vector geometry for dual-mirror optics.
Used SageMath symbolic manipulation and Taylor expansions to derive and simplify terms.
Validated the formulas against known single-mirror results and physical limiting cases.
evidence_signal: The authors report agreement with simpler analytical cases and limiting behavior checks, and find deviations up to 40% from previous methods in inclined-muon shadowing for SCT-like designs.
watch_item: The largest corrections depend strongly on telescope geometry and baffles, so the model must be implemented with the exact instrument configuration to avoid miscalibration.
paper_title: Using Muon Rings for the Calibration of the Cherenkov Telescope Array: An Analytical Solution for the Dual-Mirror Telescope Using Vector Geometry
